intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: I worked as a contractor for a federal agency. I can't say which one. But I saw some things.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Alright. What kind of things?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Documents. Redacted reports about surveillance programs that don't officially exist.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: And you read them?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Enough to know they're watching more than anyone realizes. Cell phones, smart TVs, everything.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: That's been public knowledge since Snowden. Nothing new there.

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: What Snowden leaked was the tip of the iceberg. The scope is bigger.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: People think they're paranoid until they see the proof.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: Exactly. I thought it was conspiracy talk too. Until I read the files myself.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Stay safe out there. Thanks for calling.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Thanks, Vern. People need to know.

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Former government contractor here. I've seen things I wasn't supposed to.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: What kind of things?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Surveillance programs. The real ones, not what they admit to publicly.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Such as?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Every smart device in your home is a potential listening post. Every one.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Everyone knows about smart device surveillance by now.

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: They know the surface. Not the infrastructure behind it.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: I tape over my laptop camera. Have for years.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: Smart. Most people laugh at that. They shouldn't.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: We've got {callerName} on the line from {callerLocation}. What's your story?

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Vern, I spent eight years as a contractor for a federal agency. I'm not at liberty to say which one, but I have information.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Alright, I'm listening. What kind of information?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Surveillance programs. The scope of domestic monitoring is far beyond what's been disclosed publicly.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: We've had whistleblowers before. What makes your information different?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: I worked in data analysis. Every smart device, every phone, every car with GPS - it's all being logged. Not just metadata. Content.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: This has been alleged before. Hard to verify without documentation.

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: I understand the skepticism. But the infrastructure exists. I've seen it.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: Eight years inside. That's not someone who just read something online.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: No. I was part of the machine. That's why I left.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Appreciate you coming forward. Stay safe.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Thank you, Vern. People deserve to know what's happening.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: {callerName} from {callerLocation}, you're on. I understand you've got some inside knowledge?

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Eight years as a federal contractor, Vern. I've seen how the surveillance state actually works.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Now we're talking. What can you tell us?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Every smart device in America is a potential surveillance node. Phones, TVs, refrigerators, cars. All of it feeds into centralized databases.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Centralized where? Who has access?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Multiple agencies. And private contractors like I was. The oversight is minimal at best.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Without documents, this is just testimony. Can you prove any of it?

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: I took precautions before I left. But sharing would put people at risk.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: This is why I've been saying for years - be careful what you plug in.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: You're ahead of most people. The convenience isn't worth the cost.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Thank you for the insight. This is important stuff.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Someone had to say it. Thanks for giving me a platform, Vern.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: Folks, {callerName} from {callerLocation} says they worked inside the system! Let's hear it!

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Eight years, Vern! Eight years as a federal contractor! I've seen everything!

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Tell us! What's really going on in there?!

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Total surveillance! Every smart device, every phone, every car - it's all being monitored and logged!

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Everything?! How is that even possible?!

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Server farms the size of shopping malls! AI processing billions of data points daily! It's all connected!

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Wait wait wait - you have proof of this?! Documentation?!

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: I can't share it publicly! But I saw it with my own eyes!

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: This is EXACTLY what we've been warning people about! RIGHT HERE!

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: You were right all along! The skeptics have no idea!

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Stay safe out there! And thank you for coming forward!

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Thank you, Vern! Keep spreading the truth!
